intent: Add symmetric KL divergence distillation to the original model's predictions during fine-tuning on the retain set with a cosine annealing learning rate scheduler and a small learning rate to better preserve original knowledge and improve forgetting.
rationale: Incorporating symmetric KL divergence distillation regularizes the fine-tuning on the retain set by encouraging the updated model to maintain similar output distributions as the original model, thus preserving knowledge related to retained data and preventing catastrophic forgetting. Using a cosine annealing scheduler with a small learning rate stabilizes training and helps the model converge smoothly, improving test and retain accuracy. This approach extends the current best method by adding a lightweight, effective distillation regularization inspired by previous successful runs, balancing forgetting and retention without expensive retraining.

intent: Add a few initial gradient ascent steps on the forget set using a uniform-target KL divergence loss to actively degrade forget set memorization before fine-tuning on the retain set with combined cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation to preserve retain and test accuracy.
rationale: Performing gradient ascent on the forget set with a KL divergence loss targeting a uniform distribution encourages the model to become uncertain on the forget data, effectively unlearning it. This active forgetting step before fine-tuning helps reduce the forget set's influence more strongly than fine-tuning alone. Maintaining the existing fine-tuning with cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation on the retain set ensures the model preserves its performance on retained and test data. This approach is inspired by the Fanchuan method's success in forgetting via uniform KL ascent but is kept lightweight (few iterations) to comply with runtime constraints and integrated seamlessly into the current best method's framework.

intent: Add lightweight adaptive Gaussian noise injection to convolutional weights before uniform-target KL gradient ascent on forget set to enhance forgetting, followed by the existing retain set fine-tuning with cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation to preserve retain/test accuracy.
rationale: Injecting small Gaussian noise selectively into convolutional weights prior to gradient ascent on the forget set helps perturb memorized forget-set features, boosting forgetting effectiveness without destabilizing training. Keeping the noise magnitude modest and adaptive ensures the model remains stable. Following this with the proven fine-tuning approach that combines cross-entropy and symmetric KL distillation on the retain set helps preserve retain and test accuracy. This builds on the current best method by integrating a noise-based forgetting mechanism inspired by the Seif method, which has shown forgetting improvements when combined carefully with distillation.
